topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on the screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet hum of my evening. A profile picture loaded: a familiar, sneering face I hadn't seen in fifteen years. My thumb hovered over the 'Accept' button, a hesitation tightening my chest. It felt like picking up a cold, heavy stone. I traced the outline of the name, a ghost from a time I desperately tried to outrun. A low sigh escaped my lips, a sound that seemed too large for the small apartment. Why now? The thought was a dull ache behind my eyes. I closed the app, the silence rushing back in, thick and suffocating.

[story 2]

I stared at the incoming request until the pixels seemed to swim. Every muscle in my jaw clenched. It wasn't surprise that hit me, just a deep, internal settling, like sinking into too soft sand. This person. The memory of their sharp words, the way they used to dismantle my confidence piece by piece, surfaced unbidden. My hands, resting on the worn wooden table, began to tremble almost imperceptibly. I took a slow, deliberate breath, tasting the stale air of the room. Accepting felt like opening an old, sealed box filled with sharp edges and old resentments. I didn't move, just watched the little red dot pulse insistently.

[story 3]

The phone vibrated, pulling me from the lukewarm bath. It was an invitation from someone from the past. I didn't even bother to look at the name initially; I just felt the familiar tightening around my sternum. It was exhausting, this constant background noise of old hurts resurfacing. I let the phone rest on the bedside table, its glow reflecting dimly off the closed blinds. A deep fatigue washed over me, heavy and pervasive, making even the simple act of sitting up feel like lifting lead weights. I didn't reach for it. Not yet.
